items.title: Nepal's former finance minister arrested for money laundering
items.summary: KATHMANDU: Nepal's former finance minister Bishnu Prasad Paudel was arrested late on Monday on charges of money laundering, police said, as the country's Gen Z-backed government steps up a crackdown on alleged corruption under previous administrations. Paudel, 66, a senior leader of the Communist Party of Nepal (UML) and a key figure in the former K.P. Sharma Oli government, was detained from a hotel in western Nepal, police said.

items.title: Iran open to oil supply talks with Malaysia as waiver takes effect – Envoy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 60-day waiver on the sale of Iranian crude oil in the global market has opened the door for Iran and Malaysia to further strengthen economic cooperation, particularly in the energy sector. Iranian Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said the waiver allows countries to purchase Iranian crude oil and petroleum products without restrictions, creating opportunities for deeper collaboration. "Any country now can buy oil and oil production from Iran, and there is no ban on such cooperation.

items.title: No room for excuses: Salleh says every Pan Borneo delay drains Sabah’s coffers and patience
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 23 — Inspector General of Projects Sabah Datuk Seri Dr Salleh Said Keruak reaffirmed his commitment to closely monitoring the implementation of the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway Project, stressing that every delay carries a cost to both government expenditure and public benefit. Speaking after a site visit on Tuesday with officials from the Public Works Department (JKR), contractors and relevant agencies, Salleh said several key work packages (WP) under the project were making encouraging progress and remained on track to achieve major milestones this year. WP 3, 4, 10 and 29 are on track to obtain their Certificates of Partial Completion (CPC) this year,” he said.

items.title: Johari: Sabah, Sarawak need upgraded ports to tap export potential
items.summary: Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said both states possess significant natural resources, but logistical limitations currently hinder their ability to attract larger shipping volumes and international trade routes. We have many natural resources in Sabah and Sarawak. Johari said upgrading ports would require significant investment to expand capacity and improve facilities.

items.title: Brother held after durian orchard dispute leaves sibling seriously injured
items.summary: Northeast police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received information on the incident about 3.50pm. Rozak said the victim sustained serious injuries and was receiving treatment at Penang Hospital. He said police later arrested the suspect, the victim's 62-year-old brother, about 6.30pm on the same day.

items.title: PCM rebrands as Parti Wawasan Negara, Hamzah says move boosts Perikatan’s strength and appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Parti Cinta Malaysia (PCM) has officially rebranded itself as Parti Wawasan Negara, with its de facto leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainuddin saying the move marks a new chapter for both the party and Perikatan Nasional (PN). In a statement today, Hamzah said delegates at a special party meeting on June 20 unanimously approved the name change to Parti Wawasan Negara. “The inclusion of Wawasan Negara also adds value to Perikatan Nasional’s existing strengths, particularly in advancing an agenda of change founded on competence, integrity and stability,” he said.

items.title: BNM'S International reserves at US$130.5 bil as at June 15, 2026
items.summary: BNM said the gross international reserves comprised foreign currency reserves (US$114.4 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.5 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.4 billion). Meanwhile, the central bank said its total assets amounted to RM606.30 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves including special drawing rights (RM526.90 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM1.84 billion), loans and advances (RM24.68 billion), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM35.23 billion). BNM said capital and liabilities totalled RM606.30 billion, comprising capital (RM100 million), reserves (RM199.13 billion), currency in circulation (RM183.47 billion), deposits by financial institutions (RM100.32 billion), federal government deposits (RM12.83 billion), other deposits (RM70.31 billion), Bank Negara papers (RM8.89 billion), allocation of special drawing rights (RM26.38 billion), and other liabilities (RM4.89 billion).

items.title: Govt proposes raising max traffic compound from RM300 to RM500
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is proposing to increase the maximum compound rate for traffic offences from RM300 to RM500, effective Jan 1, 2029. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the move is in line with amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 and is intended to improve compliance rather than burden the public or increase government revenue. He said among the offences subject to the new compound rate are improper display of registration numbers, using a vehicle without a valid motor vehicle licence, and driving without a valid driving licence.

items.title: LBS' Lim Hock San honoured with Asean Lifetime Achievement Award
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: LBS Bina Group Bhd group executive chairman Tan Sri Dr Lim Hock San has been honoured with the Lifetime Achievement Award 2026 – Visionary Business Leadership & Community Excellence at the recent Asean Outstanding Business Award 2026, held at the Sunway Resort Hotel in Kuala Lumpur. The accolade recognises distinguished business leaders whose lifelong contributions go beyond corporate success to generate meaningful social impact, strengthen communities and foster regional cooperation across Asean. The Lifetime Achievement Award is a fitting recognition of Lim's enduring contributions to business, community development and regional progress.

items.title: Education Ministry orders immediate school interventions for students showing mental health warning signs
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 23 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) has urged schools to carry out immediate interventions for students identified as showing signs of mental health issues to ensure their welfare and safety continue to be safeguarded. “We conduct mental health screening interventions, while efforts to strengthen the capacity of school counsellors are also being given due attention. Meanwhile, she said the Safe School Management Guidelines and the School Student Protection Policy introduced by the MOE are mandatory for all school administrators to implement without compromise.

items.title: Oct 6 hearing set for Altantuya family's judicial review bid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court will hear, on Oct 6, an application for judicial review filed by the family of Mongolian national Altantuya Shaariibuu, seeking to compel police to investigate an additional affidavit and a statutory declaration linked to her murder case. On Oct 16 last year, judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman - now a Court of Appeal judge - allowed Shaariibuu to initiate judicial review proceedings after the court was informed that the Attorney-General (A-G) had no objection to the application. The additional affidavit, dated Sept 24, 2024, and the statutory declaration, dated Oct 17, 2019, were relied upon by Azilah in support of his application to review his death sentence at the Federal Court on Oct 10 the same year.

items.title: Thai government's US$12 billion borrowing plan in legal crosshairs as growth falters
items.summary: BANGKOK: A Thai government plan to borrow 400 billion baht (US$12.2 billion) to cushion the economy from higher oil prices is facing a legal test that could define the scope of its emergency powers to help struggling households and spur economic growth. Thailand's Constitutional Court is reviewing whether the borrowing decree issued by Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's administration qualifies as an emergency measure, after accepting a petition from opposition lawmakers last month. A court ruling that disrupts the borrowing plan "could add another layer of uncertainty to the growth outlook," said Lavanya Venkateswaran, senior Asean economist at OCBC, though solid exports and some external tailwinds could support near-term momentum.

items.title: Perak Education Dept cooperates with police probe after Form One student critically injured in school fall
items.summary: IPOH, June 23 — The Perak State Education Department (JPN) is fully cooperating with police in the investigation into an incident involving a Form One student who was reported to have fallen from a building at a school yesterday. “The student is currently receiving treatment in hospital. “JPN Perak has taken the necessary measures to ensure the student’s family receives appropriate welfare assistance and support.

items.title: Aeon Credit to ride on premium bike trend
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Credit Service (M) Bhd is banking on growing demand for premium motorcycles and stronger brand visibility to drive further expansion of its motorcycle financing business in the financial year ending Feb 28, 2027 (FY27). Managing director and chief executive officer Daisuke Maeda said motorcycle financing remains one of its core growth segments, supported by stable asset quality and increasing demand across higher-value categories. He said non-performing loans in the motorcycle financing portfolio have remained stable, allowing the company to continue expanding its presence in the market.

items.title: Anwar tells Johor exco to question BN, PN over state revenue claims
items.summary: TANGKAK: Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim urged a Johor executive councillor to question the previous Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional federal administrations regarding claims that the state did not receive its fair share of revenue. Without naming the exco member, the prime minister said the allegations actually referred to events that occurred under the governance of the two coalitions before and after 2018. Yes, I am the prime minister, but the deputy prime minister is the Umno president.

items.title: Hiking boom turns deadly with 63 fatalities and over 1,000 accidents, govt moves to tighten trail safety
items.summary: Deputy Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh said statistics from the Fire and Rescue Department of Malaysia (JBPM) highlighted the need to strengthen safety measures amid the growing popularity of hiking and forest recreational activities in recent years. “JPSM has also made it mandatory to engage Forestry Mountain Guides (MGP) in 189 high-risk hiking areas. These certified guides support hiker safety, emergency response and compliance with hiking practices.

items.title: Most SMEs still in experimental stages of AI maturity, says report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nearly 70 per cent of small and medium enterprises (SMEs) remain in the experimental or opportunistic stages of artificial intelligence (AI) maturity despite growing investment and widespread use of AI tools, according to a new global report commissioned by SAS and IDC. The report, titled AI for Small and Midsized Businesses: Closing the Readiness-Reality Gap, is based on a survey of more than 1,600 leaders of SMEs across 28 countries. IDC vice-president of research Daniel-Zoe Jimenez said SMEs need to move beyond disconnected pilot projects and align their data, people and resources to realise the full value of AI.

items.title: Developer fails in bid to challenge Genting road privatisation
items.summary: GM Aero Support Sdn Bhd had sought to invalidate land titles issued over the road, arguing that the move affected access to its planned commercial development in Bentong. The dispute centred on eight parcels of land forming the main access road to Genting Highlands, which were alienated and issued titles in March 2023 by Pahang State Authority to Genting Highlands Bhd for use as a private road. It filed the suit against the Pahang Land and Mines director, the Pahang Registrar of Titles, the Bentong Land Administrator, and Genting Highlands Bhd.

items.title: Chin Tong, Cai Tung among DAP veterans dropped for Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU: Several prominent DAP figures will not defend their seats in the upcoming Johor state election. According to the candidate list announced by Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in Tangkak last night, they include Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. Liow Cai Tung, who has held the Johor Jaya seat for three consecutive terms since the 2013 general election, was also dropped, making way for Lee Wern Yiing.

items.title: Iranian ambassador highlights 60-day oil waiver as chance for Petronas and Malaysia to deepen cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The 60-day waiver on the sale of Iranian crude oil in the global market has opened the door for Iran and Malaysia to further strengthen economic cooperation, particularly in the energy sector. Iranian Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said the waiver allows countries to purchase Iranian crude oil and petroleum products without restrictions, creating opportunities for deeper collaboration. “Any country now can buy oil and oil production from Iran, and there is no ban on such cooperation.

items.title: Retirees recount losing life savings to alleged investment scams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Some 100 scam victims who gathered outside the Federal Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) today said they lost their retirement savings accumulated over decades of work. Che Kob said he invested his pension and retirement gratuity after being introduced to the scheme by friends. He said the cooperative initially paid dividends for about six months before payments stopped altogether.

items.title: Six arrested after two men slashed at Jelutong temple
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Six men have been arrested following a parang attack at a temple in Jelutong that left two men with severe injuries. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the incident occurred about 4.30am on Sunday at a Chinese temple in Jelutong. Lim said a 64-year-old Chinese temple caretaker who attempted to intervene and stop the assault was also attacked.

items.title: Police urged to expedite probes into 18 suspected scam companies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) today gathered more than 100 investment scam victims to urge police to expedite investigations into 18 companies and investment platforms allegedly linked to syndicated fraud schemes. MHO secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim said the organisation had received complaints involving hundreds of victims nationwide, with some cases dating back several years. "Victims do not just want investigation papers opened.

items.title: Peru leftist presidential candidate demands annulment of overseas vote
items.summary: LIMA: Peruvian left-wing presidential candidate Roberto Sanchez called on Monday for the annulment of overseas votes in the runoff election in which he is narrowly trailing his conservative rival. With almost all the ballots counted, right-wing candidate Keiko Fujimori held 50.11 per cent of the vote, versus Sanchez's 49.88 per cent, according to the National Office of Electoral Processes. Fujimori is ahead of Sanchez by just over 40,000 votes.

items.title: Fadhlina Sidek demands stricter school safety, guidelines enforced after student’s fatal fall in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 23 — The Education Ministry has ordered all schools nationwide to strengthen their protection and safety aspects through the implementation of mandatory school security guidelines without any compromise. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the ministry has taken drastic steps to address protection and safety issues in schools by introducing mandatory security guidelines that were announced last week. “The aspect of student protection and safety must be given serious attention by schools to ensure a safe and conducive learning environment for all.

items.title: RM25.2mil allocated for solar LED lights at 32 high-risk highway locations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has allocated RM25.2 million for the initial phase of installing solar-powered LED street lights at 32 high-risk and accident-prone locations along highway networks nationwide. Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said the initiative, under the Madani Monitoring programme, involves the installation of 3,000 light poles to enhance road users' safety at the identified locations. Also present were Works Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Azman Ibrahim, MHA chairman Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad, MHA director-general Datuk Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff and Public Works Department director-general Datuk Ibrahim Esa.

items.title: E-hailing driver remanded over assault of ex-wife outside police station
items.summary: KAJANG: Police have obtained a three-day remand order against an e-hailing driver suspected of assaulting his former wife at a public car park outside the Bandar Baru Bangi police station. District police chief Assistant Commissionet Naazron Abdul Yusof said the remand order was granted by the Bandar Baru Bangi magistrate's court today. Naazron said investigations into the motive and circumstances surrounding the incident were still ongoing.

items.title: ‘We won’t be a rubber stamp’: MPs demand parliamentary vetting for public prosecutor in constitutional split Bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Several PKR lawmakers today called on the government to expand parliamentary oversight in the planned constitutional amendments aimed at separating the offices of the Attorney General (AG) and the Public Prosecutor (PP). Among the points of contention cited is Clause 18 of the amendment, which the lawmakers said only requires the Judicial and Legal Service Commission to inform Parliament of nominated names and allow for comments. “What we must have is a bipartisan parliamentary vetting process for nominees proposed by the Judicial and Legal Service Commission, with open and transparent hearings in which Parliament on behalf of the rakyat, is able to rigorously examine nominees,” he said.

items.title: BN to name Johor candidates tomorrow?
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional is expected to unveil its candidates for the upcoming state election tomorrow morning. According to a poster believed to have been issued by the Johor BN secretariat, the candidates will be announced at the Johor Umno headquarters, with senior national and state coalition leaders expected to attend. Previously, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the coalition's candidate selection criteria centred on service, acceptability and familiarity rather than age.

items.title: 12 held in Penang over illegal online betting during World Cup crackdown
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have arrested 12 men and seized nearly RM9,000 in cash in a statewide crackdown on illegal online betting during the Fifa World Cup. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the arrests were made under Op Soga XI, a nationwide operation targeting unlicensed sports betting and online gambling activities. It was recently reported that 58 people, including four women, were nabbed for illegal sports betting and online gambling activities linked to the 2026 World Cup.

items.title: Kerjaya Prospek lands RM488mil Andaman project from E&O
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd has secured a RM488.4 million contract for the construction of a second marina-edge residence at Andaman Island. The company said its wholly-owned Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd will develop two 46-storey serviced apartment towers. Meanwhile, Kerjaya Prospek completed the acquisition of a 49 per cent stake in Aspen Vision Land Sdn Bhd to support the Aspen Vision City development in Batu Kawan, Penang.

items.title: Work hard, avoid bickering, Anwar tells PH machinery in Johor [WATCH]
items.summary: TANGKAK: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged the coalition's leaders and election machinery in Johor to avoid bickering with parties that are part of the federal government. Anwar, who is also prime minister, said PH must focus on working hard and addressing issues effectively to secure victory in the state election. In meetings, everything is fine, but when they leave, they fight," he said after announcing PH's candidates for the Johor election last night.

items.title: Topmix partners RHB, SP Setia to expand digital renovation platform myRuma
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Topmix Bhd has teamed up with RHB Bank, RHB Islamic Bank and SP Setia to expand its myRuma digital renovation platform by offering financing solutions and access to residential property buyers. Under the partnerships, RHB Bank and RHB Islamic Bank will provide home and renovation financing through a milestone-based payment framework, allowing funds to be released in stages to verified renovation professionals while improving transparency during renovation works. Separately, Topmix's collaboration with SP Setia will bring myRuma into the residential township segment through the Setia EcoHill 2 development, giving homeowners access to integrated renovation solutions and a curated network of design-and-build professionals.

items.title: Rival parties cannot use same coalition logo in same seat, says EC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Election Commission has reportedly said election candidates from rival parties cannot use the same party or coalition logo when standing in the same constituency. Berita Harian reported that EC deputy chairman Azmi Sharom said the use of a logo is limited to candidates who have been authorised by the party or coalition concerned via a formal letter. According to the report, Former EC deputy chairman Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Wan Omar said Regulation 11(2)(d) of the Election (Conduct of Elections) Regulations bars candidates from different parties from using the same logo.

items.title: PKR MPs: Give Parliament real vetting power, not just 'right to comment' on public prosecutor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Eight PKR lawmakers have called for a robust parliamentary vetting process in the proposed constitutional amendments to separate the roles of the attorney-general and public prosecutor. "What we must have is a bipartisan parliamentary vetting process for nominees proposed by the Judicial and Legal Service Commission. Wong said the group also rejects Clause 18 of the proposed amendments, which only requires the Judicial and Legal Service Commission to inform Parliament of nominees for public prosecutor and allows Parliament to submit comments.

items.title: Azalina: Malaysia well placed to lead Islamic arbitration growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is well placed to help shape the growth of Islamic arbitration as Islamic finance, halal trade, wakaf development and cross-border investments expand. She said the future of Islamic arbitration depends on talent, legal certainty and innovation. Azalina said Malaysia's Islamic banking, takaful, sukuk and Syariah-compliant investment ecosystem is now recognised internationally.

items.title: SPAN investigates drowning of maintenance worker at Saujana Perdana tower, safety checks under spotlight
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 23 — The National Water Services Commission (SPAN) is conducting investigations into a fatal accident involving a maintenance worker at the Saujana 1 water tower in Kuala Selangor on June 16. In a statement today, the commission said any party found to have failed to comply with required procedures, including Air Selangor or a SPAN permit holder, would face appropriate action. Any infringement of the Water Services Industry Act 2006 (Act 655) or relevant subsidiary legislation will be subject to appropriate action, SPAN said.

items.title: 'Water off a duck's back': Sweden's PM dodges slew of scandals
items.summary: SWEDISH Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson has been dogged by scandal throughout his four-year term, wriggling out each time with little consequence, and now faces new corruption allegations three months ahead of legislative elections. Two of the volunteers were later appointed to government positions, it said. Aftonbladet's reporter also said she was able to access Kristersson's and Ed's bedroom on the property without a security check.

items.title: Rubio faces tough task of selling Iran reset to wary Gulf allies
items.summary: ABU DHABI: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio faces a delicate mission this week pitching Washington's Iran peace deal to Gulf Arab leaders who fear excessive concessions will strengthen Teheran and reshape the region's security balance and oil flows. At issue are elements of a draft agreement that include no limits on Iran's ballistic missiles, a proposed US$300 billion reconstruction fund and provisions that could expand Tehran's regional influence and control over critical oil shipping lanes. The opinions of these nations matter to US policymakers.

items.title: Johari Ghani: Malaysia risked higher tariffs if US deal wasn't signed
items.summary: Investment, Trade and Industry Minister, Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani told Parliament today that Malaysia risks facing adverse impacts on its trade competitiveness if it does not continue engaging with the United States (US) over reciprocal tariffs and other trade measures. When the goods arrive in the US, they will impose an additional 19 per cent, which is passed on to their consumers, not us. "But the impact on us is that our products, although competitive, become uncompetitive due to the additional 19 per cent imposed by the US.

items.title: Malaysia must keep engaging US on tariffs, says Miti
items.summary: According to Berita Harian , Johari told the Dewan Rakyat that halting engagement with the US could expose Malaysia to risks and affect the country’s economy and trade. Johari said the additional tariff could make Malaysian products less competitive in the US — a key export market particularly for the E&E and semiconductor sectors. In April, Johari reportedly said Malaysia was facing several US trade actions under various American legal provisions.

items.title: Gombak police tracking man in military-style uniform, suspected replica firearm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Police are tracking down a man dressed in a military-style uniform and carrying what is believed to be a replica firearm, following a video that went viral on social media. Gombak police chief ACP Ibrahim Husin said in a statement today that the incident is believed to have occurred in the vicinity of Taman Bukit Desa, Kepong, Selangor. Members of the public with information related to the incident are urged to contact Insp S. Prakash at 012-294 0827, the Gombak district police headquarters opeQrations room at 03-612 6222, or visit the nearest police station.

items.title: Tourism minister: Rain Rave Water Festival generated RM320m in tourism revenue, drew half a million people
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Rain Rave Water Festival held on the Labour Day holiday generated an estimated RM320.4 million in overall tourism revenue, Tourism Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing told Parliament this morning. The event, which received backlash primarily from more conservative politicians, drew close to half a million visitors across all seven states it was held in. The event was held concurrently in Negeri Sembilan, Terengganu, Johor, Melaka, Kedah, Pahang and Labuan.

items.title: Loke: Malaysia must modernise ports, secure shipping routes amid global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Malaysia must build a future-ready maritime economy, anchored by resilient ports, a diversified energy architecture, innovative digital infrastructure, secure shipping corridors, and robust regional cooperation. “As global shipping routes recalibrate around geopolitical uncertainties, we have a significant opportunity to reinforce our standing as a premier maritime, logistics, and transhipment hub,” he said in his keynote address at the Global Maritime Economics Conference 2026, organised by the Maritime Institute of Malaysia here today. The conference, themed ‘Advancing Resilience and Accelerating Sustainable Maritime Growth’, explores the trends influencing global maritime economic growth amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.

items.title: Environment Ministry says it will not shield anyone from MACC probe into Japan elephant transfer
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 23 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) will provide full cooperation to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) regarding the investigation into the transfer of three Asian elephants, known as Dara, Amoi, and Kelat (DAK), from Taiping Zoo to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan. Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the ministry has taken note of the ongoing investigation and reaffirmed its commitment to the principles of transparency, accountability, and the rule of law in every aspect of the country’s wildlife management. On June 18, wildlife rights group Hak Asasi Hidupan Liar Malaysia (Hidup) called on the MACC to investigate the transfer of the three elephants.

items.title: Why refugees like the Rohingya and other asylum seekers find themselves in a legal limbo in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Malaysia is home to over 215,000 refugees and asylum seekers. This includes 193,824 Rohingya, 15,774 Chin, and 33,002 other ethnic minorities. However, Malaysia not being a signatory of the 1951 Refugee Convention or its 1967 Protocol leaves this vulnerable community in a legal grey area.

items.title: Bersatu’s status in Perikatan ‘not discussed’, says Muhyiddin after emergency coalition meeting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has reportedly said his party’s position in Perikatan Nasional (PN) was not discussed during the coalition’s emergency meeting last night. The meeting comes amid tensions between Bersatu and PAS, after PAS on June 8 decided to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, although both parties remain in PN. PN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar reportedly said Pejuang and Parti Cinta Malaysia’s entry into PN was agreed to unanimously.

items.title: Perikatan approves Pejuang and PCM as new members, seat talks commence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Perikatan Nasional Supreme Council (PN) last night decided to approve the applications of Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang) and Parti Cinta Malaysia (PCM) as new members of the coalition. Apart from the announcement, PN chairman Datuk Seri Ir Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar also said PN would finalise the seat distribution for the Johor state election soon. The Election Commission (EC) has set June 27 as nomination day, while July 7 is the early voting day and July 11 is the polling day.

items.title: Malaysian Bar ‘not optimistic’ over plan to separate AG, public prosecutor roles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 23 — The Malaysian Bar has reportedly expressed concern over the government’s proposed implementation of the separation of the roles of the Attorney-General and public prosecutor. According to the New Straits Times , Malaysian Bar president Anand Raj said the Bar was “not optimistic” that the final outcome would reflect what it had hoped for. “All we need to say is, the separation of Attorney-General and public prosecutor in theory and in concept is good.

items.title: It’s flexible: Diesel subsidy recipients can use quota for diesel or RON95 petrol, says finance minister II
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 23 — The government has set a base eligibility limit of up to 200 litres per month for eligible individuals under the BUDI Madani Diesel (BUDI Diesel) programme, with the quota to be used flexibly for the purchase of subsidised diesel or RON95. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said this approach ensures that subsidies are given to eligible recipients based on their actual usage, regardless of whether the fuel used is diesel or RON95. He said this gives recipients flexibility in managing their monthly fuel consumption.

items.title: Admissions based on merit, not money, say public varsity chiefs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 23 — The Committee of Vice-Chancellors and Rectors of Public Universities (JKNCR) yesterday stressed that public higher learning institutions have never engaged in the practice of selling admission slots or providing entry through the “backdoor” to any party. “Admissions to public universities are always subject to academic requirements, merit, programme capacity, accreditation requirements and approval from the relevant university authorities,” the statement said. “This continuous increase proves that the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) is always expanding access to public higher education and not reducing it as claimed,” the statement said.

items.title: Pakatan names full Johor election candidate list: Maszlee Malik for Puteri Wangsa, former state assembly speaker for Larkin
items.summary: TANGKAK, June 23 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) announced its full list of 56 candidates for the Johor election last night, ending speculation over which candidates it would field. Former Johor state legislative assembly speaker Suhaizan Kayat — who is also Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) vice-chairman and Pulai MP — was named as PH’s candidate for the Larkin state seat. For the upcoming Johor election, PH is fielding 20 PKR candidates, 19 Amanah candidates and 17 DAP candidates.

items.title: Boy, 13, critical after fall at Ipoh secondary school
items.summary: Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the Ipoh district police headquarters received information regarding the incident via the MERS 999 hotline at 2.49pm. As such, the public is advised not to speculate or spread unverified information regarding this incident,” Mohd Alwi said in a statement last night. Mohd Alwi said police will conduct a transparent and thorough investigation.

items.title: Anwar: Johor got RM16b back from Putrajaya, more than it contributed
items.summary: He said the Federal government, however, has returned RM16 billion to Johor through various development projects, operating allocations and related programmes. RM16 billion for Johor,” he said at the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate announcement ceremony for the Johor state election here last night. The prime minister said Johor received about RM6 billion to RM7 billion annually in operating expenditure under the previous government.

items.title: ‘Know our limits’: Keep royal institution out of Johor election contest, says PM Anwar
items.summary: TANGKAK, June 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the upcoming Johor election must strictly remain a contest between political parties, without involving the royal institution or the Malay rulers. Also present were PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, its deputy chairman Anthony Loke and the coalition’s vice-chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu. Earlier, Anwar also stressed the importance of good relations between the federal government and the country’s royal institution, especially in Johor.

items.title: Above our rooftops: A beginner’s guide to the Low Altitude Economy
items.summary: The concept is known as the Low Altitude Economy (LAE) — an emerging sector that uses low-level airspace, generally below 1,000 metres, as a new economic zone for transport, logistics, public services and industry. The Low Altitude Economy typically refers to economic activities that use low-level airspace, typically below 1,000 metres — much lower than commercial aircraft, which operate at around 9,000 to 13,000 metres — through technologies such as drones, unmanned aircraft and future electric air vehicles. This includes delivery drones, drones used for agriculture, security and infrastructure inspection, mapping and surveying, emergency and medical services, and more recently, pilotless aircraft known as Electric Vertical Take-Off and Landing (eVTOL) aircraft, or “flying taxis”.

items.title: Finance minister II: Too early to raise Budi95 quota back to 300 litres
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — The government is not yet considering restoring the Budi95 monthly fuel quota from 200 litres to 300 litres, saying it is still assessing the impact of recent developments in the Middle East. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the ceasefire announced recently was still in its early stages and it remained unclear whether it would hold long enough to ease global oil market uncertainties. Amir Hamzah was responding to a question on whether Putrajaya was considering reinstating the Budi95 quota to 300 litres a month following recent declines in fuel prices.

items.title: RM2.10 diesel from July 1: Who qualifies, how it works and how much fuel you can buy
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 22 — The government will roll out a new MyKad-based diesel subsidy system from July 1, allowing eligible Malaysians to buy diesel at RM2.10 per litre while moving all diesel prices to market rates nationwide. The move marks the biggest change to the Budi Diesel programme since targeted diesel subsidies were introduced in June 2024, replacing monthly cash assistance for individual recipients with direct fuel subsidies at the pump. The government estimates around 700,000 private diesel vehicle owners will qualify.

items.title: Johor Regent grants an audience with PM Anwar, meeting reaffirms close ties between Putrajaya and state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 22 — The Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, granted an audience to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Istana Bukit Tunku here earlier today. He reaffirmed that the federal government will continue to maintain good relations and close cooperation with its Johor counterpart. “Tunku Mahkota Ismail and I also agreed to continue to maintain good relations, in addition to strengthening close cooperation between the federal government and the Johor government,” he said in a statement issued today.

items.title: Police finalising investigation paper on man who claimed Tok Batin hid hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: IPOH, June 22 — Police are finalising the investigation papers regarding the defamation case on the Tok Batin of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu via a live broadcast on the Tik Tok platform regarding the discovery of the missing female climber, Jaslinda Saludin. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the investigation paper has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor for further action. “We have sent the investigation paper to the public prosecutor and we need to update the investigation.
